title: Temperature Sensor Failure
shortDescription: The built-in temperature sensor is not responding or reading incorrectly.
fullDescription: Error E100 on Ecobee thermostats indicates the internal temperature sensor has failed or is reading values outside the expected range. The thermostat cannot accurately control HVAC operation without a working sensor.
symptoms: Temperature shows extreme or stuck value
Remote sensors working but thermostat sensor not

fixSteps.title: Restart
fixSteps.instruction: Press and hold the power button for 10 seconds to force restart the Ecobee.

fixSteps.title: Check Placement
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the thermostat is not in direct sunlight or near a heat source.

fixSteps.title: Use Remote Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If available, set a remote sensor as the primary temperature source in settings.

fixSteps.title: Factory Reset
fixSteps.instruction: Navigate to Main Menu > Settings > Reset > Reset All Settings.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists after reset — the thermostat may need warranty replacement.

faq.q: Can I use remote sensors as backup?
faq.a: Yes, Ecobee allows you to set SmartSensors as the primary temperature source.
Ecobee offers a 3-year limited warranty on their thermostats.
Main Menu > Settings > Reset > Reset All Settings.

title: HVAC Equipment Not Responding
shortDescription: The Ecobee sent a command but the HVAC system did not respond.
fullDescription: Error E200 means the Ecobee thermostat is sending signals to the HVAC equipment but not detecting the expected response. The equipment may have a fault, or there could be a wiring issue.

fixSteps.title: Check Breaker
fixSteps.instruction: Verify all HVAC breakers are on at the electrical panel.

fixSteps.title: Check Wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the Ecobee from the wall plate and verify all wires are firmly inserted.

fixSteps.title: Check Equipment Fuse
fixSteps.instruction: At the furnace or air handler, check the low-voltage fuse on the control board.

fixSteps.title: Test Equipment
fixSteps.instruction: Jumper R and W (heat) or R and Y (cool) at the equipment to test directly.

whenToCallPro: If the equipment does not respond to a direct jumper test.

faq.a: Bypassing the thermostat by connecting wires directly to test if the equipment works independently.
Usually a short in thermostat wiring — could be from a wire touching another terminal.

faq.q: Does the Ecobee need a C wire?
faq.a: Yes, Ecobee requires a C wire. A PEK (Power Extender Kit) is included as an alternative.

title: WiFi Connection Lost
shortDescription: The Ecobee has lost its WiFi network connection.
fullDescription: Error E300 indicates the Ecobee thermostat cannot connect to the configured WiFi network. Smart features, remote access, and weather data will be unavailable until connectivity is restored.
symptoms: E300 or WiFi icon with exclamation

fixSteps.title: Check Router
fixSteps.instruction: Verify your WiFi router is on and other devices can connect.

fixSteps.title: Reconnect WiFi
fixSteps.instruction: On the Ecobee, go to Main Menu > Settings > WiFi and reconnect.

fixSteps.title: Check 2.4GHz
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure your router is broadcasting a 2.4GHz network — Ecobee does not support 5GHz.

fixSteps.title: Move Router
fixSteps.instruction: If signal is weak, move the router closer or add a WiFi extender.

whenToCallPro: Not needed — WiFi is not required for basic thermostat operation.

faq.a: Yes, basic heating and cooling work without WiFi. Smart features require connectivity.
2.4GHz has better range and penetration through walls, which is important for thermostat placement.

faq.q: Can I use a WiFi extender?
faq.a: Yes, a WiFi extender or mesh system can help if signal is weak at the thermostat location.

title: Calibration Error
shortDescription: The thermostat temperature calibration is off and needs correction.
fullDescription: Error E400 indicates the Ecobee's temperature reading does not match actual room temperature. This can happen over time or after installation in a poor location.

causes.cause: Thermostat near heat source or draft

symptoms: Room feels different than displayed temp

fixSteps.title: Check Location
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the thermostat is not near windows, vents, kitchens, or direct sunlight.

fixSteps.title: Compare Temperature
fixSteps.instruction: Place a separate thermometer next to the Ecobee and compare after 30 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Adjust Correction
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Installation > Thresholds > Temperature Correction and adjust.

fixSteps.title: Use SmartSensor
fixSteps.instruction: Place a SmartSensor in the room for more accurate readings.

whenToCallPro: Only if you need to relocate the thermostat to a better position.

faq.q: How much can I adjust the temperature correction?
Where is the best thermostat location?
faq.a: Interior wall, 5 feet high, away from vents, windows, kitchens, and direct sunlight.
Yes, they provide temperature data from multiple rooms for better averaging.

title: PEK (Power Extender Kit) Communication Error
shortDescription: The Power Extender Kit at the HVAC equipment is not communicating with the thermostat.
fullDescription: Error E500 indicates the Ecobee Power Extender Kit (PEK), which provides C wire functionality when no C wire is available, has lost communication with the thermostat.

causes.cause: PEK wiring loose at HVAC board

fixSteps.title: Check PEK Connections
fixSteps.instruction: At the HVAC equipment, verify all PEK wires are firmly connected.

fixSteps.title: Verify PEK Wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Compare PEK connections to the installation diagram — ensure SAC, DIM, ACC terminals are correct.

fixSteps.title: Check PEK LED
fixSteps.instruction: Look for the green LED on the PEK module — it should be solid green when working.

fixSteps.title: Reinstall PEK
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and reinstall the PEK module following the Ecobee installation guide.

whenToCallPro: If PEK reinstallation does not resolve the error — consider running a proper C wire instead.

faq.a: The Power Extender Kit provides C wire functionality by repurposing existing thermostat wires.

faq.q: Should I install a real C wire instead?
faq.a: A dedicated C wire is always more reliable than the PEK.

faq.q: Is the PEK included with Ecobee?
faq.a: Yes, the PEK is included in the box with Ecobee thermostats.

title: Software/Firmware Error
shortDescription: The Ecobee firmware has encountered a critical error and needs to be restarted.
fullDescription: Error E600 indicates a firmware crash or critical software error on the Ecobee thermostat. This can be caused by a failed update, corrupted memory, or a software bug.
symptoms: Display shows error and reboot loop

fixSteps.title: Force Restart
fixSteps.instruction: Press and hold the reset button for 15 seconds until the Ecobee logo appears.

fixSteps.title: Remove from Base
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the thermostat off the wall plate for 30 seconds, then reattach.

fixSteps.title: Factory Reset
fixSteps.instruction: If accessible, go to Main Menu > Settings > Reset > Reset All Settings.

fixSteps.title: Contact Ecobee
fixSteps.instruction: If stuck in a reboot loop, contact Ecobee support for firmware recovery instructions.

whenToCallPro: If the thermostat is stuck in a boot loop after factory reset — may need warranty replacement.

faq.q: Will a factory reset fix E600?
faq.a: Usually yes — it clears corrupted data and restores default firmware.
Yes, a factory reset removes all custom settings, but they sync from the cloud if WiFi is restored.
Yes, software-related failures within the warranty period are typically covered.
